mapping.a_element: spatial variations of local pairing temperature due to mesoscopic disorder fluctuations
mapping.b_element: periodic potentials translated by half a period in supersymmetric quantum mechanics

mapping.a_element: probability distribution function of pairing temperatures and superconducting gaps
mapping.b_element: identical band spectra of isospectral potentials

mapping.a_element: superconducting islands and metallic regions with suppressed density of states
mapping.b_element: zero modes supported by both isospectral potentials in periodic supersymmetry

brief_justification: Paper A studies mesoscopic disorder fluctuations leading to spatial variations in superconducting properties, while Paper B focuses on supersymmetric quantum mechanics with periodic potentials and isospectrality. The underlying mechanisms—disorder-driven fluctuations in a superconductor versus symmetry-driven spectral properties in quantum mechanics—are fundamentally distinct, despite superficial similarities in spectral or spatial variation descriptions.
